footer.previewWarning: This branch is running on a preview database — accounts can be wiped at any time. Do not rely on data being saved anywhere other than locally. If payments are activated, do not enter real payment information.

about.title: About HackTheTrack

offline.heading: Works Offline & Can Be Installed
offline.body: HackTheTrack is a fully offline-capable web application. Once loaded, it works without an internet connection — perfect for the track. You can install it like a native app on your phone, tablet, or computer by using the "Install" option in your browser menu (or the prompt that appears at the bottom of the page).

data.heading: Your Data Stays on Your Device
data.body: All data processing happens entirely in your browser, and everything is saved locally on your device by default. Cloud storage is entirely optional — nothing leaves your device unless you create an account and turn on sync.

community.heading: Community Track Database
community.body: Don't see your track? You can define custom track and course layouts in the editor, then submit them to the site-wide database for everyone to use. Submissions are reviewed before being added.

oss.heading: Free & Open Source
oss.body: Every local feature in HackTheTrack is completely free, and the source code is open and available on GitHub. Optional cloud storage has a free tier; larger storage and AI coaching are paid add-ons that cover server and model costs — but all local features will always remain free.

features: Multi-format file support (NMEA, UBX, VBO, MoTeC, AiM CSV + XRK/XRZ, Alfano, Dove, Dovex)
Automatic track & course detection within 5 miles
Automatic driving direction detection (forward/reverse)
Waypoint mode — lap timing anywhere, no track needed
Interactive race line map with speed heatmap
Braking zone detection & visualization
Automatic lap detection via start/finish line
3-sector split timing with optimal lap
Pro graph view with multi-series telemetry charts
G-G diagram (friction circle) for grip-usage analysis
Distance or time chart axis — laps line up by track position
Reference lap overlay & pace delta comparison
Multi-lap overlay — compare laps across the map & graphs at once
Overlay laps from past sessions & other loggers (auto drift-aligned)
Lap snapshots — fastest lap saved per engine & course for comparison
Video sync with telemetry playback
9 overlay gauge types (digital, analog, graph, bar, bubble, map, pace, sector, lap time)
MP4 video export with overlays & audio (H.264 + AAC)
Vehicle profiles & setup sheet management
Frozen setup history — every session keeps the setup it ran
Session notes per file
BLE device integration (DovesDataLogger)
Device track sync over Bluetooth
Over-the-air firmware updates — flash your logger straight from the app
Custom track & course editor with community submissions
Local weather lookup
Dark & light mode
PWA — installable & fully offline

browserCompat.title: Browser Compatibility
browserCompat.description: Feature support for your current browser.
browserCompat.note: All core features work across modern browsers. Some advanced features work best in Chrome or Edge.

supportedFiles.title: Supported File Formats
supportedFiles.description: All parsing is done locally in your browser — nothing is uploaded.

dove.name: Dove CSV
dove.body: Simple CSV with millisecond Unix timestamps, GPS data, RPM, and hardware accelerometer readings. The native format of DovesDataLogger. Extension: .dove

dovex.name: Dovex (Extended Dove)
dovex.body: Extended Dove format with a 4096-byte metadata header containing session info (driver, course, lap times) followed by standard Dove CSV GPS data. Extension: .dovex

xrk.name: AiM XRK / XRZ (Binary)
xrk.body: AiM's native binary telemetry from MyChron / SoloDL loggers, including the zlib-compressed .xrz variant. Parsed entirely in your browser by libxrk compiled to WebAssembly — fast and fully offline, like every other format. Extensions: .xrk, .xrz

iracing.name: iRacing Telemetry (Binary)
iracing.body: iRacing's native binary telemetry export — the same data the sim's live irsdk API serves, no third-party CSV/MoTeC conversion needed. Read directly in your browser (fully offline): GPS position, speed and altitude drive the map and laps, with throttle, brake, gear, steering, RPM, water/oil temp and native lateral/longitudinal g along for the ride. Extension: .ibt

nmea.name: NMEA / CSV (Tab-Delimited)
nmea.body: Tab-delimited CSV with NMEA sentences — the legacy format used by our earlier custom dataloggers. Extensions: .nmea, .csv, .txt

ubx.name: u-blox UBX Binary
ubx.body: Binary NAV-PVT messages from u-blox GPS receivers. Extension: .ubx

vbo.name: Racelogic VBO
vbo.body: Racelogic VBOX and RaceBox export format. Extension: .vbo

motecLd.name: MoTeC LD Binary
motecLd.body: Binary data from MoTeC data loggers and sim racing exports (ACC, iRacing, etc.). Extension: .ld

motecCsv.name: MoTeC CSV
motecCsv.body: CSV exports from MoTeC i2 Pro analysis software. Extension: .csv

alfano.name: Alfano CSV
alfano.body: CSV exports from the Alfano ADA app. Extension: .csv

aimCsv.name: AiM MyChron CSV
aimCsv.body: CSV exports from Race Studio 3 (RS2Analysis style) for MyChron 5/6. Extension: .csv
